title: Micromeles yuana
taxonomy: Micromeles yuana is classified within the plant family Rhamnaceae, also known as the buckthorn family. This family consists of approximately 600 species of flowering plants. Micromeles yuana is a species of flowering plant that is native to China. The genus Micromeles is closely related to the genus Ziziphus, which includes the jujube tree. Further classification of Micromeles yuana may include its family rank and order within the Rhamnaceae family.

However, its medicinal uses require further scientific investigation to fully understand its potential benefits.

intro: Micromeles yuana, also known as Yuanyang, is a plant species native to the mountainous regions of southern China. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in alleviating symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Micromeles yuana include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare tea, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
